{{Quote|The smaller craft are the combat vehicles. Stick with the five transports.|General Carlist Rieekan during the Rendezvous on Barkhesh|<ref name="Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)">[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]</ref>}}
'''Armed transports''' were long [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]] capable of carrying large amounts of cargo. They were usually armed with a single [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]].

The [[Barkhesh Resistance]] employed five of the transports, escorted by three [[combat vehicle]]s, in a [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] to transport supplies to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. The transports were [[Rendezvous on Barkhesh|escorted]] by the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]].

[[File:BarkheshConvoy_2.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The Barkhesh convoy.]]
Later, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] employed the vehicles as decoys for any possible rescue missions on the planet [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. They were rigged to emit Human life readings to deflect attention away from a nearby [[armored hovertrain]] that was actually carrying the prisoners and give it time to reach its destination, though this attempt didn't work.

{{Veh-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The armed transports appear in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]. In the ''Rescue on Kessel'' mission, the Imperials use the vehicles as decoys; the player has the option of hunting them or proceeding to the hovertrain.
